expected: A valid reset token succeeds once; a second confirm with the same token returns 400 reset_token_used, while other reset tokens for the same user are invalidated after password change.
redEvidence: corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts apps/api/src/authRegistrationRoutes.test.ts -> RED: repeated confirm after a successful password reset returned { error: 'invalid_reset_token' } instead of { error: 'reset_token_used' }. The broader package-script run also surfaced unrelated pre-existing failures in apiDocs/agentExecute.
greenEvidence: corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts apps/api/src/authRegistrationRoutes.test.ts -> 1 file / 14 tests passed.
fixApplied: The password-reset confirm transaction now preserves the consumed token row as evidence for stable repeat-token classification and deletes only other reset tokens for the same user. Persistence exposes deleteOtherPasswordResetTokensByUserId for this exact policy.
regressionRisk: Low-medium. Security posture stays intact because other reset tokens are still invalidated and sessions are still deleted. Consumed reset-token rows now remain until normal data retention/cleanup; if long-term cleanup is added, it must not remove rows before the repeat-token UX/security window expected by the product.
